+title: Migrate kanban-cli onto the shared generator; delete its private cli_gen.rs
+---
+## What
+Switch kanban-cli to the shared `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen` generator (card B) and delete the now-duplicate `apps/kanban-cli/src/cli_gen.rs`. This is a pure refactor ‚Äî identical CLI behavior, one fewer copy of the engine.
+
+Changes:
+- `apps/kanban-cli/src/main.rs`: remove `mod cli_gen;` (line 8). Replace the two call sites ‚Äî `cli_gen::build_commands_from_schema(schema)` in `build_cli` (line 54) and `cli_gen::extract_noun_verb_arguments(...)` in `dispatch` (used at `apps/kanban-cli/src/main.rs:335`) ‚Äî with `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen::build_commands_from_schema` / `::extract_noun_verb_arguments`.
+- Keep feeding the FULL schema in-process: `generate_kanban_mcp_schema(operations)` at `main.rs:34` must continue to produce the full schema (with `x-operation-schemas`) for the generator. After card D switches the per-tool wrapper to slim, ensure kanban-cli calls the `_full` variant (e.g. `generate_kanban_mcp_schema_full`) so its command tree keeps per-op precision. If E lands before D, no change needed yet; if after, point it at `_full`.
+- Delete `apps/kanban-cli/src/cli_gen.rs`.
+- `apps/kanban-cli/Cargo.toml` already depends on `swissarmyhammer-operations` (:27) ‚Äî no dep change. `once_cell` (:44) may become unused if the interner moved out; drop it if so.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-cli/src/cli_gen.rs` is deleted and `mod cli_gen;` removed from `main.rs`.
+- [ ] `kanban` CLI produces the identical noun/verb/arg command tree as before (same nouns, verbs, per-op required flags).
+- [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-cli` succeeds with no unused-dep/import warnings.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add a golden command-tree test in `apps/kanban-cli` (e.g. `tests/cli_tree.rs`): build the clap command via the shared generator from the kanban full schema, walk noun‚Üíverb‚Üíargs, and assert the expected nouns (`board`, `task`, `column`, `tag`, ‚Ä¶) with each verb's scoped required args ‚Äî mirroring the assertions ported in card B but exercised through kanban-cli's actual `build_cli`.
+- [ ] A regression check that `kanban task move` (or a known per-op-required case) enforces that op's required field and does NOT accept the global union.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-cli` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write the golden-tree test against the shared generator first, confirm it passes with the shared module, then delete the private `cli_gen.rs`.
\ No newline at end of file

+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write the golden-tree test against the shared generator first, confirm it passes with the shared module, then delete the private `cli_gen.rs`.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-07 15:58)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:62` ‚Äî `build_cli` (plus its `global_flag` helper) is the third verbatim copy of the same CLI-assembly skeleton ‚Äî global flags, then schema-driven op subcommands via `cli_gen::build_commands_from_schema`, then a fixed list of lifecycle subcommands. Three identical orchestrators means a flag or wiring fix has to be made in three places and will drift. Extract a shared `build_cli(name, about, schema, lifecycle_subcommands)` (and a `global_flag` helper) into a shared crate alongside the existing `swissarmyhammer_cli_completions`, and have code-context/shelltool/kanban/avp all call it, passing only name/about.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:119` ‚Äî The lifecycle subcommand builders `serve_subcommand`/`init_subcommand`/`deinit_subcommand`/`doctor_subcommand`/`completion_subcommand` are duplicated verbatim across all three CLIs, differing only in the about/long_about strings ‚Äî a parameterizable difference, not a real fork. Move these builders into the shared CLI-scaffold module, parameterized by the per-tool about strings (e.g. `serve_subcommand(about: &str)`), and call them from each CLI.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:191` ‚Äî The install-target helper trio `install_target_arg`/`target_value`/`target_to_scope` is copied byte-for-byte from the other CLIs. These have no per-tool variation at all, so there is nothing to fork ‚Äî every CLI maps the same `project|local|user` strings to the same `InitScope`. Hoist these three into the shared scaffold crate (or reuse mirdan's `InitScope` conversion path that `apps/avp-cli/src/install.rs:17` already provides) and delete the local copies.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:195` ‚Äî The valid lifecycle-scope set {"project","local","user"} is enumerated as raw string literals in two parallel code paths that a human must keep in lockstep: the clap whitelist `value_parser(["project", "local", "user"])` (line 195) and the decoder `target_to_scope`'s match arms (lines 257-259). Adding or renaming a scope requires editing both; if the match arm is forgotten, clap accepts the value and the program panics at runtime (line 260) instead of failing to compile. A canonical typed representation already exists ‚Äî the `InstallTarget` ValueEnum in cli.rs (variants Project/Local/User with a Display impl yielding exactly these three strings) ‚Äî so this is duplicated, not single-sourced, data. Single-source the set: reuse the existing `InstallTarget` ValueEnum as the arg's `value_parser` (clap `EnumValueParser`, as already done for `Shell` on line 184) and parse straight into it via `get_one::<InstallTarget>`, then `impl From<InstallTarget> for InitScope`. The clap whitelist and the scope mapping then derive from one enum and cannot drift; the runtime `panic!` arm disappears because the type is exhaustive.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:320` ‚Äî `run_completion` is a verbatim third copy of the completion dispatcher ‚Äî it just calls `print_completion(build_cli(schema), shell)` and maps the result to an exit code. Identical control flow already exists in the other two CLIs. Once `build_cli` and `print_completion` are centralized, fold `run_completion` into the shared scaffold so each CLI invokes one shared completion path.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:162` ‚Äî `install_target_arg` is a byte-for-byte third copy of the same helper in two sibling CLIs. Triplicated CLI scaffolding means a fix to the target arg (e.g. a new scope value) has to be made in three places and will silently drift. Hoist `install_target_arg` (and the `["project","local","user"]` value set) into a shared CLI-lifecycle helper crate that all three binaries call; delete the per-binary copies.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:170` ‚Äî `target_value` is an exact duplicate of the same accessor in the other two CLIs ‚Äî pure duplication with zero per-binary variation. Move `target_value` into the same shared lifecycle helper as `install_target_arg`; the two are always used as a pair.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:184` ‚Äî The valid lifecycle-target set and its target-string ‚Üí InitScope mapping is a table split across two hand-maintained sites that must stay in lockstep. `install_target_to_scope` matches on "project"/"local"/"user" (lines 184-188), while `install_target_arg`'s `value_parser` independently lists the same three strings (line 166). Add or rename a scope and you must edit both, or the parser admits a value the match panics on ‚Äî the panic arm at line 188 exists precisely to absorb that drift. Define one table as the single source of truth, e.g. `const TARGETS: &[(&str, InitScope)] = &[("project", InitScope::Project), ("local", InitScope::Local), ("user", InitScope::User)];` (or a `FromStr`/`as_str` pair on `InitScope` in `swissarmyhammer_common::lifecycle`). Derive the `value_parser` from its keys and look up the scope from the same table, so the panic arm becomes unreachable-by-construction rather than a drift backstop.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:300` ‚Äî `run_completion` is a near-identical third copy across the CLIs ‚Äî it rebuilds the command tree and writes the completion script with the same logic everywhere. The completion path will diverge per-binary on the next change. Parameterize a single `run_completion(build_cli, shell)` (and a shared `completion_subcommand`) on the binary name; `swissarmyhammer_cli_completions::print_completion_for` already takes the name, so the wrapper is the only thing left to share.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:3` ‚Äî The crate-level `Commands:` doc list (lines 3-7) is stale after this change: it omits the `completion` subcommand and the schema-driven shell-operation subcommands (`command execute`, `processes list`, `history grep`, `lines get`, `process kill`) that are the whole point of this refactor. Crate docs that under-list the actual command surface mislead anyone reading the binary's entry point. Add `- `shelltool completion <shell>`: Generate shell completion scripts` and a line noting the schema-driven shell operations (e.g. ``shelltool <noun> <verb>``: run a shell operation) to the `Commands:` block.
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs:24` ‚Äî `build_tool_arguments` returns `Result<_, String>` and collapses the underlying error via `format!("Error processing arguments: {}", e)`; the validator's error-handling rule asks application code to use `anyhow::Result<T>` so the `Error::source()` chain is preserved rather than flattened into a single Display string. Return `anyhow::Result<serde_json::Map<...>>` and replace the `format!` mapping with `.context("processing arguments")`. This keeps the source chain intact; `report_error_and_exit` can render it with `{:#}`. If the binary deliberately standardizes on `String` errors at this seam, leave as-is ‚Äî this is a nit, not a blocker.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs:475` ‚Äî New test fn `serde_defaulted_fields_excluded_from_required_signatures` omits the `test_` prefix that every other test in this module uses, breaking the file's established naming pattern and making the test harder to spot/scan among its peers. Rename to `test_serde_defaulted_fields_excluded_from_required_signatures` to match the surrounding convention (cf. the parallel test `test_serde_default_field_not_required` added in macro_expansion.rs).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-operations-macros/src/lib.rs:198` ‚Äî has_serde_default only inspects field-level attributes, so a container-level #[serde(default)] (which makes every field optional) is not detected ‚Äî those non-Option fields would still be emitted as required, the exact false-positive this change exists to prevent, just at the container scope. Either document this scope limit in the doc comment ("detects field-level serde defaults only; container-level #[serde(default)] is not considered"), or plumb the container attributes into the required computation so a struct-level default also marks fields optional.
\ No newline at end of file
